---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
##library(readxl)
##library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size45[.center[
Chen, A., Nyhan, B., Reifler, J., Robertson, R., & Wilson, C. (2021). Exposure to Alternative & Extremist Content on YouTube (The Belfer Fellowship). *Anti-Defamation League*.
]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. ...

<br>

More cutting-edge research on the internet and extremism!

Today we analyze recent research on the impact of YouTube on the spread of extremism.

WHAT ARE THE BIG CONCLUSIONS OF THE REPORT?

(SLIDE)



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size35[
"Overall, our findings indicate that YouTube plays an important role in exposing people to potentially harmful content."

"We do not find clear evidence that YouTube frequently exposes people with neutral or mixed views on issues such as race to alternative or extremist content."

"Instead, the audience for videos from alternative or extremist channels is dominated by people who already have high levels of racial resentment (more than 90% of views for both types)."
]

???

SO, WHAT DOES THIS MEAN IN ENGLISH?

- DOES YOUTUBE EXPOSE PEOPLE TO EXTREMIST CONTENT?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[**Sample Selection**]]

<br>

.size60[
1. Nationally representative survey

2. YouTube viewing data
]

???

Let's now talk methods.

To get the data they need the researchers ran a nationally representative survey and collected YouTube viewing data.

Let's evaluate these two sources.

HOW DID THEY DO THIS AND HOW WELL DID THEY DO IT?

<br>

Now check the appendix results for details about the survey sample.

HOW CONFIDENT ARE WE THAT THIS IS A REPRESENTATIVE SAMPLE?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[**Variable Measurement**]

<br>

.size60[
Identifying problematic or harmful content on YouTube
]

???

Let's now evaluate how the researchers classified YouTube content to identify extremist content.

PROS AND CONS OF THIS EFFORT?

(Note the "Limitations" section in the report)



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[**Results**]

<br>

.size60[
How strong are the effects they identify?
]

???



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/14_2-youtube_dark.jpg')
background-size: 90%
background-position: center

.size55[.center[.textwhite[**Does YouTube Increase Political Violence by Non-State Actors?**]]]

???

BOTTOM LINE, DOES THIS PAPER DEMONSTRATE A LINK BETWEEN YOUTUBE USAGE AND POLITICAL VIOLENCE BY NON-STATE ACTORS? WHY OR WHY NOT?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/14_2-Jan6th_Riot.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: top
class: center

.size70[.textred[**For Friday**]]

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

.size35[.content-box-red[**Political Violence by Non-State Actors in the US**]]

???

Friday we discuss the elephant in the room.

The 2024 election is almost certainly our next HUGE test for political violence by non-state actors in this country.

So, let's talk about it!
